title: Private key PEM not zeroized on drop
description: In persona.rs, `generate_keypair()` returns the private key as a plain `String` (via `.to_string()` on the Zeroizing wrapper at line 15). The `rsa` crate's `to_pkcs8_pem()` returns a `zeroize::Zeroizing<String>`, but calling `.to_string()` copies the bytes into a new `String` that has no zeroize-on-drop. This plain `String` is then returned from `generate_keypair()`, inserted into SQLite, and later fetched back in `get_private_key()` as another plain `String`. The key material lives in heap memory for the lifetime of those `String` values and is never explicitly overwritten. On process crash or swap, the PEM can appear in core dumps or swap files. Fix: keep the `Zeroizing<String>` from `to_pkcs8_pem()` throughout the call chain, or use the `secrecy` crate's `SecretString` type. Do not call `.to_string()` until the last possible moment (e.g. when passing to SQLx bind which needs `&str`).
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
